body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds docs, dataset manifests, tooling and run artifacts; introduces a BaseAgentRunner abstraction with lifecycle/timing/stats; computes ε-robustness in reliability code; adds task variant YAMLs; provides figure/table generation and dataset upload scripts; updates CLI/help, Git ignore rules, and many run result artifacts. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Docs & Governance**
`CHANGELOG.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `DATASHEET.md`, `README.md`, `paper/ED_TRACK_GUIDELINES.md`|Add changelog, contribution guide, datasheet, HF paper guidance, and small README wording changes.| |**Repository Infra & Config**
`.gitignore`, `Dockerfile`, `Makefile`, `config/llm/gemini-3-flash.yaml`|Refine ignore rules (runs/logs, paper LaTeX artifacts), add Dockerfile for build/test, Makefile targets (install/test/lint/eval/docker), and a Gemini LLM YAML config.| |**Dataset Manifests & HF Tooling**
`croissant.json`, `scripts/populate_croissant_hashes.py`, `scripts/hf_dataset_card.md`, `scripts/upload_hf_dataset.sh`|Add Croissant dataset manifest, script to populate SHA256 hashes, Hugging Face dataset card, and HF upload/staging script.| |**Evaluation Abstraction & Runners**
`evaluation/base_runner.py`, `evaluation/_archive/runners/...`, `evaluation/_archive/runners/langchain_runner.py`, `python/agentdisruptbench/cli.py`|Introduce `RunnerConfig` and `BaseAgentRunner` ABC (setup/teardown/__call__/stats); small LangChain agent import/signature update and CLI help string change.| |**Reliability & Task Variants**
`python/agentdisruptbench/core/reliability.py`, `python/agentdisruptbench/tasks/builtin/variants.yaml`|Add helper to derive base task IDs and compute ε-robustness; add 18 task variants YAML across domains.| |**Result Analysis & Baselines**
`scripts/generate_figures.py`, `scripts/generate_tables.py`, `scripts/run_baselines.sh`, `scripts/run_baselines.sh`|Add CLI scripts to generate publication figures and LaTeX tables from run JSONs, plus a baseline orchestration shell script.| |**Paper Assets (LaTeX)**
`paper/tables/table_overall_success.tex`, `paper/tables/table_per_domain.tex`, `paper/tables/table_recovery_metrics.tex`|Add LaTeX table sources for overall success, per-domain, and recovery metrics.| |**Run Artifacts & Reports**
`runs/...` (many `runs//*`), `rac_out.txt`|Add numerous run result/summary/report JSON and MD artifacts; remove archived `rac_out.txt`.| |**Utilities & Analysis Helpers**
`evaluation/compare_runs.py`, `evaluation/show_run.py`, `scripts/generate_tables.py`|Update run labeling to include runner, improve run viewer to load task registry and show ground truth, prefer newer disruption/latency keys, and lift agent-output truncation.| |**Misc Scripts**
`scripts/populate_croissant_hashes.py`, `scripts/upload_hf_dataset.sh`, `scripts/run_baselines.sh`|Add utilities for dataset hash population, HF upload staging, and baseline execution orchestration.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller as Caller participant Runner as BaseAgentRunner participant Tools as ToolProvider participant Stats as StatsStore Caller->>Runner: __call__(task, tools) Note right of Runner: lazy setup / reset metrics Runner->>Runner: if not setup -> setup() Runner->>Stats: reset per-task metrics Runner->>Runner: start timer Runner->>Tools: invoke tool calls (as needed) Tools-->>Runner: tool responses / errors Runner->>Runner: run_task(task, tools) Runner->>Stats: update tokens/time/api-calls/recovery info Runner->>Runner: stop timer Runner-->>Caller: return result (string) ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - **Kavirubc/AgentDisruptBench#5**: Overlaps on evaluation runner and LLM/config surface—related to runner/LLM wiring. - **Kavirubc/AgentDisruptBench#3**: Related to reliability computation changes in `core/reliability.py` and metrics/task schema modifications. - **Kavirubc/AgentDisruptBench#7**: Related to `evaluation/show_run.py` runtime/renderer updates. ## Poem > 🐰 I hopped through manifests, YAML and charts, > I stitched up runners and measured their parts, > Croissants hashed, tables aligned in rows, > Logs and scripts gathered where resilience grows, > A tiny hop—now the bench quietly starts.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 79.31% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title directly summarizes the main changes: adding evaluation methodology enhancements and unifying benchmark runners through new documentation, infrastructure, and configuration files. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feature/hugginface-base-runners`
--- Thanks for using [CodeRabbit](https://coderabbit.ai?utm_source=oss&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=Kavirubc/AgentDisruptBench&utm_content=8)! It's free for OSS, and your support helps us grow. If you like it, consider giving us a shout-out.
❤️ Share - [X](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A&url=https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/share?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai) - [Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/submit?title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&text=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai&mini=true&title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&summary=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code)

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues
